name: Network Sniffing Activity
notes:
This rule detects network sniffing activities.

Covered from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Timur Zinniatullin, oscd.community
SIGMA Title: Network Sniffing
SIGMA Description: Network sniffing refers to using the network interface on a system to monitor or capture information sent over a wired or wireless connection. An adversary may place a network interface into promiscuous mode to passively access data in transit over the network, or use span ports to capture a larger amount of data.


Confidence: MEDIUM

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success, Audit.Object Load Success
when the event matches "Process Full Full Name" is not NULL AQL filter query
when the event matches ( LOWER("Process Full Full Name") LIKE 'tshark.exe' AND LOWER("Command") LIKE '% -i %' ) OR LOWER("Process Full Full Name") LIKE 'windump.exe' AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Network Sniffing Activity on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success, Audit.Object Load Success
AND when the event matches "Process Full Full Name" is not NULL AQL filter query
AND when the event matches ( 	LOWER("Process Full Full Name") LIKE 'tshark.exe' 	AND LOWER("Command") LIKE '% -i %' ) OR LOWER("Process Full Full Name") LIKE 'windump.exe' AQL filter query
RED_list: Event description: Detects network sniffing activities.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
